answer:
We first find the cost of goods sold. With a 45% margin, the cost percentage is 55%, so cost of goods sold = 55% × $72,600 = $39,930.  

Then, cost of goods available = beginning inventory ($30,156) + purchases ($28,300) = $58,456.  

Subtracting cost of goods sold gives ending inventory: $58,456 – $39,930 = $18,526.  

\boxed{G}
